articles.title: Bitcoin consolidates near $77K ahead of Fed decision; $80K breakout could trigger $1.2B short squeeze
articles.content: Bitcoin consolidated near the $77,000 mark on Wednesday ahead of the US Fed's rate decision, while experts believe that the next key trigger might be the April CPI report. The cryptocurrency was trading at the $77,159 mark.
articles.excerpt: Bitcoin consolidated near the $77,000 mark on Wednesday ahead of the US Fed's rate decision, while experts believe that the next key trigger might be the April CPI report. The cryptocurrency was trading at the $77,159 mark.

articles.title: OnEMI Technology IPO opens tomorrow: GMP, price band, and Analyst view
articles.content: OnEMI Technology Solutions' Rs 925.92 crore IPO opens April 30, closing May 5, with a price band of Rs 162-171. The digital lending platform, operating under Kissht and Ring, aims to strengthen its subsidiary's capital base. The issue shows modest grey market interest, trading at a Rs 4 premium.
articles.excerpt: OnEMI Technology Solutions' Rs 925.92 crore IPO opens April 30, closing May 5, with a price band of Rs 162-171. The digital lending platform, operating under Kissht and Ring, aims to strengthen its subsidiary's capital base. The issue shows modest grey market interest, trading at a Rs 4 premium.

articles.title: Crude shock: Ajay Bagga on how $90 oil could dent India Inc's earnings and test government's fiscal nerve
articles.content: India is bracing for a major energy cost shock. Soaring crude oil prices are set to impact corporate profits, consumer spending, and government finances. The nation's import bill for crude and gas is projected to rise significantly. Experts warn that the pain will eventually be felt by consumers and the government. The aviation sector is particularly vulnerable.
articles.excerpt: India is bracing for a major energy cost shock. Soaring crude oil prices are set to impact corporate profits, consumer spending, and government finances. The nation's import bill for crude and gas is projected to rise significantly. Experts warn that the pain will eventually be felt by consumers and the government. The aviation sector is particularly vulnerable.

articles.title: HUL Q4 Preview: Volume recovery or margin illusion? Here's what the consumer giant may show in earnings
articles.content: Hindustan Unilever is set to report its March quarter results. Growth is anticipated from increased sales volumes. The demerger of its ice cream business will impact headline figures. Analysts expect steady revenue growth. Margins will be a key focus, with underlying pressures potentially masked by the business carve-out. Investors await insights on rural demand and input costs.
articles.excerpt: Hindustan Unilever is set to report its March quarter results. Growth is anticipated from increased sales volumes. The demerger of its ice cream business will impact headline figures. Analysts expect steady revenue growth. Margins will be a key focus, with underlying pressures potentially masked by the business carve-out. Investors await insights on rural demand and input costs.

articles.title: Citius TransNet InvIT lists at 5% premium over IPO price. Check details
articles.content: Units of Citius TransNet Investment Trust listed at a 4.5% premium, debuting at Rs 104.50 against the issue price of Rs 100. The Rs 1,105-crore fresh issue saw strong demand with 10.42x subscription. The InvIT manages 3,400+ lane-km of road assets, offering stable toll and annuity revenue streams.
articles.excerpt: Units of Citius TransNet Investment Trust listed at a 4.5% premium, debuting at Rs 104.50 against the issue price of Rs 100. The Rs 1,105-crore fresh issue saw strong demand with 10.42x subscription. The InvIT manages 3,400+ lane-km of road assets, offering stable toll and annuity revenue streams.

articles.title: India’s Real Estate Will Meet the Reality of Agentic AI: Andy Mukherjee
articles.content: India’s IT slowdown, driven by AI-driven automation and shrinking outsourcing demand, is beginning to hit real estate, mortgages, and employment in tech-heavy cities. With firms like Infosys Ltd. and HCL Technologies Ltd. tightening hiring, banks are rethinking underwriting norms as job risks rise.
articles.excerpt: India’s IT slowdown, driven by AI-driven automation and shrinking outsourcing demand, is beginning to hit real estate, mortgages, and employment in tech-heavy cities. With firms like Infosys Ltd. and HCL Technologies Ltd. tightening hiring, banks are rethinking underwriting norms as job risks rise.

articles.title: UAE’s exit could reshape OPEC+ oil supply dynamics: Peter Cardillo
articles.content: The UAE's reported exit from OPEC+ is sparking concerns about the alliance's long-term cohesion, though immediate market disruption is limited. Market experts suggest this development could signal deeper structural issues, potentially leading to increased global oil supply and price collapses once geopolitical tensions subside.
articles.excerpt: The UAE's reported exit from OPEC+ is sparking concerns about the alliance's long-term cohesion, though immediate market disruption is limited. Market experts suggest this development could signal deeper structural issues, potentially leading to increased global oil supply and price collapses once geopolitical tensions subside.

articles.title: Vedanta demerger explained: Record date, how much money can you make and should you invest in buy 1, get 4 offer?
articles.content: Vedanta’s upcoming demerger will split its businesses into four new listed entities, with April 29 as the last likely date to buy shares for eligibility. While the move is expected to unlock long-term value, recent stock gains suggest limited short-term arbitrage opportunity ahead of the restructuring.
articles.excerpt: Vedanta’s upcoming demerger will split its businesses into four new listed entities, with April 29 as the last likely date to buy shares for eligibility. While the move is expected to unlock long-term value, recent stock gains suggest limited short-term arbitrage opportunity ahead of the restructuring.

articles.title: Taiwan overtakes Canada to become 6th largest stock market as AI demand lifts TSMC m-cap to $1.8 trillion
articles.content: Taiwan has overtaken Canada to become the world’s sixth-largest stock market, driven by a surge in AI-linked stocks and a sharp rally in TSMC. Strong semiconductor demand, robust earnings and regulatory easing have boosted valuations, highlighting the growing dominance of technology-heavy markets over resource-driven economies.
articles.excerpt: Taiwan has overtaken Canada to become the world’s sixth-largest stock market, driven by a surge in AI-linked stocks and a sharp rally in TSMC. Strong semiconductor demand, robust earnings and regulatory easing have boosted valuations, highlighting the growing dominance of technology-heavy markets over resource-driven economies.

articles.title: Adani Energy Solutions surges 63% in 3 months fueled by seasonality, outlook. But why brokerages are cautious?
articles.content: With the onset of summer, shares of Adani Energy Solutions have attracted strong investor interest, rallying over 63% in just three months. This makes it the second-best performer within the Adani Group, trailing only its power sector peer, Adani Power, which has delivered slightly higher returns of around 67% over the same period.
articles.excerpt: With the onset of summer, shares of Adani Energy Solutions have attracted strong investor interest, rallying over 63% in just three months. This makes it the second-best performer within the Adani Group, trailing only its power sector peer, Adani Power, which has delivered slightly higher returns of around 67% over the same period.

articles.title: AI battle heats up, OPEC fractures: Arnab Das on the two biggest market stories right now
articles.content: Global markets face two major shifts: intense AI competition sees Anthropic challenging OpenAI's dominance, raising questions about AI's monetization. Simultaneously, the UAE's exit from OPEC weakens Saudi Arabia's oil price control, signaling a move towards independent market strategies. According to global market strategist Arnab Das, these structural changes promise significant economic and investment landscape transformations.
articles.excerpt: Global markets face two major shifts: intense AI competition sees Anthropic challenging OpenAI's dominance, raising questions about AI's monetization. Simultaneously, the UAE's exit from OPEC weakens Saudi Arabia's oil price control, signaling a move towards independent market strategies. According to global market strategist Arnab Das, these structural changes promise significant economic and investment landscape transformations.

articles.title: Vijay Kedia-backed Websol Energy shares jump 45% in six days. What's driving the rally?
articles.content: Websol Energy System shares surged 5% to the upper circuit for the third day, marking a 45% rally in six days. Strong quarterly earnings, with net profit soaring 158% and revenue up 132%, coupled with ace investor Vijay Kedia's fresh purchases, have significantly boosted investor confidence and the stock's performance.
articles.excerpt: Websol Energy System shares surged 5% to the upper circuit for the third day, marking a 45% rally in six days. Strong quarterly earnings, with net profit soaring 158% and revenue up 132%, coupled with ace investor Vijay Kedia's fresh purchases, have significantly boosted investor confidence and the stock's performance.

articles.title: Silver rebounds Rs 1,300, gold at Rs 1.50 lakh ahead of US Fed decision, Iran conflict uncertainty. Key levels for today
articles.content: Gold and silver prices edged higher on MCX as investors await the US Federal Reserve’s policy decision amid ongoing Iran-related tensions. While inflation concerns from elevated oil prices support bullion, rising rate expectations cap gains. Analysts expect continued volatility and advise caution before taking fresh positions in precious metals.
articles.excerpt: Gold and silver prices edged higher on MCX as investors await the US Federal Reserve’s policy decision amid ongoing Iran-related tensions. While inflation concerns from elevated oil prices support bullion, rising rate expectations cap gains. Analysts expect continued volatility and advise caution before taking fresh positions in precious metals.

articles.title: GRSE shares soar 16% after strong Q4; net profit jumps 24% YoY to Rs 303 crore
articles.content: Shares of state-run Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers (GRSE) rallied as much as 16% to their day’s high of Rs 3,339 on the BSE on Wednesday, after it reported a net profit of Rs 303 crore for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, up 24% from Rs 244 crore in the same period last year.
articles.excerpt: Shares of state-run Garden Reach Shipbuilders & Engineers (GRSE) rallied as much as 16% to their day’s high of Rs 3,339 on the BSE on Wednesday, after it reported a net profit of Rs 303 crore for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, up 24% from Rs 244 crore in the same period last year.

articles.title: REC shares dip over 4% as Q4 net profit slips 22% to Rs 3,375 crore: Why Motilal Oswal still remains bullish
articles.content: Shares of Rural Electrification Corporation (REC) slipped over 4% after reporting a 22% YoY fall in Q4 net profit to Rs 3,375 crore. Despite weak disbursements and modest loan growth, brokerage Motilal Oswal maintained its ‘Buy’ call, citing attractive valuations and stable spreads.
articles.excerpt: Shares of Rural Electrification Corporation (REC) slipped over 4% after reporting a 22% YoY fall in Q4 net profit to Rs 3,375 crore. Despite weak disbursements and modest loan growth, brokerage Motilal Oswal maintained its ‘Buy’ call, citing attractive valuations and stable spreads.

articles.title: From Adani to Birla: India Inc promoters pump in $4 billion to buy own stocks amid selloff
articles.content: After two years of significant equity selling, Indian promoters have reversed course in 2026, investing over $4 billion into their companies as valuations normalize post-market correction. This shift, led by groups like Adani and GMR, signals renewed confidence in long-term prospects, particularly in asset-heavy sectors.
articles.excerpt: After two years of significant equity selling, Indian promoters have reversed course in 2026, investing over $4 billion into their companies as valuations normalize post-market correction. This shift, led by groups like Adani and GMR, signals renewed confidence in long-term prospects, particularly in asset-heavy sectors.

articles.title: Emmvee Photovoltaic shares climb 7%, Jefferies raises target price to Rs 350
articles.content: Emmvee Photovoltaic shares rose after a strong Q4FY26 performance, with revenue up 62% YoY and PAT surging 89%. Results beat estimates, and Jefferies reiterated a “Buy” rating, citing a solid order book and strong growth visibility despite a slight margin dip.
articles.excerpt: Emmvee Photovoltaic shares rose after a strong Q4FY26 performance, with revenue up 62% YoY and PAT surging 89%. Results beat estimates, and Jefferies reiterated a “Buy” rating, citing a solid order book and strong growth visibility despite a slight margin dip.

articles.title: Vedanta demerger record date is May 1, but why is today the last date to buy?
articles.content: Vedanta's demerger record date is May 1, a market holiday, making April 29 the last day to buy shares for eligibility. Shareholders will receive one share each of Vedanta Aluminium Metal, Talwandi Sabo Power, Malco Energy, and Vedanta Iron and Steel for every Vedanta share held. This restructuring aims to unlock significant shareholder value by creating distinct, sector-specific entities.
articles.excerpt: Vedanta's demerger record date is May 1, a market holiday, making April 29 the last day to buy shares for eligibility. Shareholders will receive one share each of Vedanta Aluminium Metal, Talwandi Sabo Power, Malco Energy, and Vedanta Iron and Steel for every Vedanta share held. This restructuring aims to unlock significant shareholder value by creating distinct, sector-specific entities.

articles.title: US Stock Market: AI spending drives chipmakers higher while software stocks falter
articles.content: In an otherwise volatile year for technology investors, one strategy has clearly stood out is buying semiconductor stocks while betting against software companies.
articles.excerpt: In an otherwise volatile year for technology investors, one strategy has clearly stood out is buying semiconductor stocks while betting against software companies.

articles.title: Hindustan Zinc dividend alert! Last date to buy shares for Rs 11 dividend, do you own?
articles.content: Hindustan Zinc has announced an interim dividend of Rs 11 per share, with April 30th being the record date. Investors must purchase shares by today to be eligible for this payout. This move follows strong Q4 earnings, with net profit soaring 68% year-on-year. The company, a leader in zinc, lead, and silver production, has a history of rewarding shareholders.

This move follows strong Q4 earnings, with net profit soaring 68% year-on-year. The company, a leader in zinc, lead, and silver production, has a history of rewarding shareholders.
